background.experience: moving critical materiel between commands while preserving custody records and fallback routes
background.attention_bias: tracks who held authority at each handoff, what changed after failures, and whether exit instructions remain executable
background.counter_bias: rejects branded service continuity as proof of owned custody

readers.thesis: Artifact sovereignty emerges when every release handoff preserves immutable identity, custody evidence, and an executable exit route independent of the current registry operator.
observations: The trajectory repeatedly replaces provider identity with project-owned manifests, gates, and recovery evidence.
The current package scripts still encode hosted-provider operations, making a neutral registry boundary a necessary migration seam.
uncertainties: The complete private conversation corpus was unavailable.
No live project-controlled registry endpoint participated in this run.

background.experience: validating long-lived archives across storage migrations and incompatible repository implementations
background.attention_bias: maps immutable identifiers, protocol guarantees, fallback semantics, and silent format degradation
background.counter_bias: does not equate successful retrieval by tag with verifiable content custody

readers.thesis: An OCI registry becomes an acceptable commodity only after the system verifies digest equality, content-discovery capability, transport policy, and neutral export semantics.
observations: OCI distribution supplies a standardized migration boundary rather than a sovereignty guarantee.
Referrers discovery determines whether signatures, SBOMs, and provenance remain attached and discoverable across registry changes.
uncertainties: Backend replication and garbage-collection behavior remain implementation-specific.
The gate does not yet test upload, deletion, backup restoration, or cross-registry copy.

agreements: Both readers reject tags and provider accounts as artifact authority.
Both require digest-bound verification before promotion.
disagreements: Reader A prioritizes custody handoffs and operator-independent exit procedures; Reader B prioritizes protocol-level conformance and metadata attachment integrity.
omissions_detected: No live dual-registry replication benchmark ran.
No offline OCI-layout export was restored.
revisions_after_discussion: Reader A adds referrers discovery to custody evidence because detached provenance can be lost during migration.
Reader B adds operator handoff and offline export to the conformance boundary because protocol support alone does not establish recoverability.

notes_added: A registry qualifies as commodity infrastructure only after exact digest custody and attached-evidence discovery pass an executable gate.
OCI compatibility creates an exit surface; independent exports, replicas, and restore drills create recovery authority.
notes_challenged: Successful image pull establishes artifact sovereignty.
Registry replication under one administrative control plane creates an independent recovery path.

privacy.review: Contains synthetic fixtures, protocol capability metrics, public technical abstractions, and explicit coverage limits only.
